claim: The ITV Benchmark (Is This Viable) is a personalized use-case specific test suite using Bun, Promptfoo, and Ollama to quickly assess whether on-device language models meet your exact requirements
actionable: Set up the ITV Benchmark framework to test local language models against your specific use cases before productionizing them
source_title: ZERO Cost AI Agents: Are ELMs ready for your prompts?

claim: Personal standards for ELMs should include: accuracy 80%+ on your test suite, speed 20+ tokens/second minimum, memory consumption under 32GB, and context window 32k minimum
actionable: Define your own personal standards for accuracy, speed, memory, and context window before evaluating efficient language models for production use
source_title: ZERO Cost AI Agents: Are ELMs ready for your prompts?

claim: The cost of running prompts approaches zero when using on-device ELMs, fundamentally changing the business model for agentic tools and products
actionable: Design agentic systems with the assumption that prompt costs will approach zero as on-device models improve, enabling higher usage intensity and new product models
source_title: ZERO Cost AI Agents: Are ELMs ready for your prompts?

claim: Llama 3 8B reached top 20 on LMSYS Chatbot Arena within a week of release and is likely to see context window improvements from 8k to 32k+ through community fine-tuning
actionable: Watch for open source model improvements like extended context windows from community variants rather than waiting for official releases
source_title: ZERO Cost AI Agents: Are ELMs ready for your prompts?

claim: JSON response support is a must-have feature for agentic workflows when using on-device models; vision support is nice-to-have but not critical
actionable: Prioritize on-device models with strong JSON parsing and response formatting capabilities for agent work
source_title: ZERO Cost AI Agents: Are ELMs ready for your prompts?

claim: Promptfoo's assertion types like contains_all, equals, and custom checks allow precise testing of model outputs against exact expectations, making test suites repeatable
actionable: Use Promptfoo's assertion system to define precise success criteria for each test case rather than relying on vague output inspection
source_title: ZERO Cost AI Agents: Are ELMs ready for your prompts?

claim: Comparing local models against a control cloud model (like GPT-3.5) in benchmarks provides a meaningful baseline for evaluating on-device model readiness
actionable: Include a high-performing cloud model (like GPT-3.5-turbo) as a control group when benchmarking local ELMs to establish performance targets
source_title: ZERO Cost AI Agents: Are ELMs ready for your prompts?

claim: As on-device ELM performance improves, the efficiency frontier shifts from three classes (fastest/cheapest/worst, slowest/most expensive/best, cloud) to new speed/accuracy trade-offs
actionable: Think about model selection in terms of speed vs. accuracy trade-offs for local models separately from cloud model paradigms as ELM options expand
source_title: ZERO Cost AI Agents: Are ELMs ready for your prompts?

claim: The prompt is the fundamental unit of programming and knowledge work; as prompt costs approach zero, capabilities approach infinity
actionable: Design systems with the mindset that prompting will become the primary interface for building systems, not coding or configuration
source_title: ZERO Cost AI Agents: Are ELMs ready for your prompts?
